text: Sprouted coconuts are one of my favorite snacks, so today we're gonna crack one open and try it. The best place to find a sprouted coconut is at the base of a mature coconut palm that has lots of fallen older coconuts. It can take up to six months for them to sprout, but with the right conditions it'll eventually happen, and the best tasting ones are the ones that have just started to sprout. Back at the house I removed the outer husk, and now it's time to crack it open. The best way to do this is by hitting it with the back end of a big knife. You don't want to use the sharp end otherwise you're gonna dull your knife, and be sure to do this over a bowl because sometimes there's leftover coconut water. When you think of a coconut you might think of it being hollow, but once it sprouts it fills with a spongy inside commonly referred to as a coconut apple. These are so different from the coconuts that you'll see for sale at the grocery store. Not only do they have the nice airy texture that just melts in your mouth, but they have just a hint of coconut with a sweet flavor that's just delicious. And the insides of these also have lots of coconut oil. So if you ever get the chance to try a sprouted coconut I would highly recommend it. And get outside and see what you can find.
